content.full_text:
City of Grover Beach

Mayor - Lowell Forister
Mayor Pro-Tem - Fred Munroe
Councilman - Gene Gates
Councilman - Peter Keith
Councilman - Ron Arnoldsen

December 2, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street - Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

To Whom It May Concern:

I have recently been elected as a Council Member with the City of Grover Beach. Currently I have a dental business in the City and provide services to many City employees, residents, and business owners in the City. After reviewing the Political Reform Act with City staff, I felt it necessary to seek advice and clarification from the FPPC concerning my business arrangments.

It is my understanding that income from my dental business would be exempt from the $250 income limit for conflict under Section 87103.5 of the Political Reform Act. Specifically, I understand that my dental business would qualify as a "service" under this section.

I would also like to seek clarification from you concerning the use of surplus campaign funds. Under Section 89519 of the Political Reform Act, I understand that I may use surplus campaign funds for civic organizations. Specifically, I would like to use the funds to pay for new street signs for the City to reflect the recent voter-approved name change. In this case, the contribution of surplus funds would be paid to the City for subsequent purchase of the new signs.

Thank you for your assistance in responding to my concerns. I am eager to obtain your advice, as I just assumed my seat of office on December 1, 1992.

Sincerely,

Ronald P. Arnoldsen D.D.S

RONALD P. ARNOLDSEN, DDS
COUNCIL MEMBER

c.c.: City Administrator
City Attorney

154 South 8th Street ♦ P.O. Box 365 ♦ Grover Beach, CA 93483 ♦ (805) 473-4567 ♦ FAX (805) 489-9657

Specifically, I would like to use the funds to pay for new street signs for the city to reflect the recent voter-approved name change. In this case, the contribution of surplus funds would be paid to the city for subsequent purchase of the new signs.
sections.question_synthetic: A newly elected council member seeks clarification on whether income from his dental business is exempt from the $250 income limit under Section 87103.5, and whether he can use surplus campaign funds to pay the city for new street signs under Section 89519.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a council member to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice sections present. Document contains clear questions regarding Political Reform Act sections 87103.5 and 89519. Some OCR errors present but overall document is legible.

embedding.summary: Newly elected Council Member Ronald P. Arnoldsen requests FPPC advice on two matters: whether his dental business income qualifies for exemption from the $250 income limit under Section 87103.5, and whether surplus campaign funds can be used to pay for city street signs under Section 89519.
